name: Ch'ao Ming Hsia P'ien B1
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Prawns or large shrimp, uncooked
1 cup Peanut or other salad oil
1 tbsp Peanut or other salad oil
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------MARINADE-------------------------
1 tablespoon Cornstarch
1 teaspoon Salt
1 tablespoon Dry sherry
1 pinch White pepper
----------------------SEASONING SAUCE----------------------
1/4 cup Chicken broth
1/4 cup Mushroom juice (from soaked mushrooms)
3/4 teaspoon Salt
1 1/4 teaspoons Cornstarch
1 tablespoon Dry sherry
Shell and de-vein the prawns. Wash and dry with paper towels.
Slice each prawn into halves lengthwise. Add prawns to Marinade, tossing to coat. Cover, marinate at least 1/2 hour in refrigerator.
Soak mushrooms in hot water for 20 minutes. Reserve juice for sauce; discard stems and cut caps in halves.
Cut baby sweet corn into halves lengthwise. Set aside.
Heat 1 cup oil in wok or skillet on high heat. Add prawns; stir-fry 15 seconds. Remove, drain off oil (reserve oil for stir-frying other seafoods).
Heat remaining 1 cup oil in wok or skillet on high heat. Add scallion, mushrooms, water chestnuts, snow peas, and baby sweet corn. Stir-fry 1 minute.
Stir in Seasoning Sauce. Cook, stirring constantly until sauce thickens. Add prawns, mix well. Serve immediately.
Marinade: Combine ingredients in a mixing bowl and mix well.
Seasoning Sauce: Combine ingredients in a small bowl.
